surface.name: Send Your First Message, on-net SMS between two Telnyx numbers
surface.selection_basis: Workshop selection policy (2026-07-19): commit to the fastest and most commonly used documented route. Prefer local/no-account when offered; otherwise the vendor's first-listed or recommended quickstart. Treat Sign up as one opaque step when form fields are not public. Selected the official Messaging guide that claims about five minutes and tests between two Telnyx numbers with no carrier registration required.

alternatives_considered.name: Python SDK first SMS guide
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Marketing resource that points back to portal setup; the developer Messaging guide is the first-party five-minute path.

alternatives_considered.name: 10DLC / toll-free verification paths
alternatives_considered.reason_not_primary: Required for many external US routes, but the selected guide avoids carrier registration by testing on-net between two Telnyx numbers.

entry_point.developer_intent: Create a Telnyx account, enable messaging on two numbers, and send a first SMS through the Messaging API.
assumed_prior_state: No Telnyx account, numbers, messaging profile, or API key exist.
curl is available for the documented send request.

documented_first_success.official_milestone: Send your first SMS using the Telnyx Messaging API
documented_first_success.normalized_outcome: A POST to /v2/messages with a Bearer API key queues an SMS from one Telnyx number to another and returns a successful message payload.
documented_first_success.observable_completion_signal: The API accepts the send request for the documented Hello, world! text between the two configured Telnyx numbers.

boundary_evidence.official_label_or_terminal_state: The guide frames completion as sending the first SMS through the /messages endpoint after numbers, profile, and API key setup.

documented_first_success.why_this_is_the_boundary: This is the terminal action of the official five-minute Send Your First Message guide.

prerequisites.requirement: A client that can send an authenticated HTTPS POST, such as curl.

primary_path.action: Open Send Your First Message in the Telnyx developer docs.
primary_path.output: The five-minute Messaging path is visible.
primary_path.success_signal: Prerequisites and numbered steps are shown.

primary_path.action: Create a Telnyx account through the official signup entry linked from the guide, then sign in to Mission Control.
primary_path.output: An authenticated Mission Control session exists.
primary_path.success_signal: The portal is reachable after signup.
primary_path.failure_or_wait: Signup form fields are not fully enumerated in public docs; treat signup as one opaque documented gate.

primary_path.action: Purchase two Telnyx phone numbers for on-net messaging tests.
primary_path.input: Numbers search and buy in Mission Control
primary_path.output: Two numbers are available on the account.
primary_path.success_signal: Both numbers appear under My Numbers.

primary_path.action: Open Messaging, create a messaging profile, and assign both numbers to that profile.
primary_path.output: Both numbers are messaging-enabled through the profile.
primary_path.success_signal: Each number's Messaging Profile dropdown shows the selected profile.

primary_path.action: Open API Keys and copy or create an API key.
primary_path.output: A usable API key value is available.
primary_path.success_signal: The key can be pasted into the Authorization header.

primary_path.action: POST to https://api.telnyx.com/v2/messages with Bearer auth, from and to set to the two Telnyx numbers in E.164, and text Hello, world!.
primary_path.output: Telnyx accepts the outbound message request.
primary_path.success_signal: The API returns a successful message response payload.

branches.path: Include publicly accessible media URLs in the request body.
branches.effect_on_first_success: Changes payload fields but keeps the same /messages endpoint terminal.

friction_gates.description: Account creation is required and is recorded as one opaque signup step because interactive form fields are not fully public.
Purchasing numbers may incur documented Mission Control charges depending on number type and region.

uncertainties.question: What exact signup fields, KYC prompts, and post-submit screens appear when creating a free Telnyx account?
uncertainties.impact: Account creation is recorded as one opaque step; intermediate signup UI cannot be reconstructed atomically from public docs.
uncertainties.reason_unresolved: The Messaging guide requires an account but does not enumerate the interactive signup form sequence.
